# Composition Patterns

**Generated by**: design-nexus v3.0.0-enhanced  
**Purpose**: Document when to nest components and component composition relationships  
**Scope**: Single component validation (Button) — full system rules would include all component combinations

---

## Core Composition Principle

**Prefer composition over configuration** — Instead of creating many component variants, compose simple components together to create complex UI.

**Example (Good — composition):**
```tsx
<Button>
  <Icon name="download" />
  Download Report
</Button>
```

**Example (Bad — variant explosion):**
```tsx
<Button variant="with-download-icon">Download Report</Button>
```

**Why composition is better:**
- Fewer variants to maintain
- More flexible (any icon, any text, any order)
- Easier to understand (what you see is what you get)
- Follows component model of React, Vue, Svelte

---

## Button Composition Patterns

### 1. Button + Icon

**Pattern**: Add visual context or recognition to button actions

**✅ Recommended (composition):**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Download, Trash2, Plus } from 'lucide-react'

<Button>
  <Download className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
  Download Report
</Button>

<Button variant="destructive">
  <Trash2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
  Delete Account
</Button>

<Button>
  <Plus className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
  Add Item
</Button>
```

**❌ Current implementation (variant-based):**
```tsx
// This design system has "with icon" as a variant — DON'T DO THIS
<Button variant="with icon">Download Report</Button>
```

**Why composition wins:**
- Icon can be placed before or after text
- Icon can be any component (not just specific icons)
- No new variant needed for each icon

---

### 2. Button + Tooltip (Icon-Only Buttons)

**Pattern**: Provide accessible labels for icon-only buttons

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Tooltip, TooltipContent, TooltipTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tooltip'
import { Settings } from 'lucide-react'

<Tooltip>
  <TooltipTrigger asChild>
    <Button size="icon" variant="ghost" aria-label="Settings">
      <Settings className="h-4 w-4" />
    </Button>
  </TooltipTrigger>
  <TooltipContent>
    <p>Open Settings</p>
  </TooltipContent>
</Tooltip>
```

**Why this pattern is important:**
- Icon-only buttons are ambiguous without labels
- Tooltips provide context on hover/focus
- `aria-label` ensures screen reader accessibility
- `asChild` prop allows Tooltip to wrap Button without extra DOM node

**⚠️ Accessibility requirement**: ALWAYS include `aria-label` on icon-only buttons, even with tooltips (tooltips may not be available to screen readers).

---

### 3. Button + Dropdown Menu (Split Buttons)

**Pattern**: Primary action with secondary options in dropdown

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuTrigger } from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu'
import { ChevronDown } from 'lucide-react'

<div className="flex">
  <Button onClick={handlePrimaryAction}>Save</Button>
  <DropdownMenu>
    <DropdownMenuTrigger asChild>
      <Button size="icon" className="ml-px">
        <ChevronDown className="h-4 w-4" />
      </Button>
    </DropdownMenuTrigger>
    <DropdownMenuContent>
      <DropdownMenuItem>Save and Continue</DropdownMenuItem>
      <DropdownMenuItem>Save as Draft</DropdownMenuItem>
      <DropdownMenuItem>Save as Template</DropdownMenuItem>
    </DropdownMenuContent>
  </DropdownMenu>
</div>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Primary action is immediately accessible (one click)
- Secondary actions are discoverable but not prominent
- Visual grouping indicates related actions

---

### 4. Button + Badge (Notification Count)

**Pattern**: Show notification count or status on button

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge'
import { Bell } from 'lucide-react'

<Button variant="ghost" className="relative">
  <Bell className="h-5 w-5" />
  {notificationCount > 0 && (
    <Badge variant="destructive" className="absolute -top-1 -right-1 h-5 w-5 p-0 flex items-center justify-center rounded-full">
      {notificationCount}
    </Badge>
  )}
</Button>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Badge draws attention to actionable items
- Absolute positioning keeps button size consistent
- Conditional rendering prevents empty badges

**Accessibility**: Include `aria-label` with count:
```tsx
<Button aria-label={`${notificationCount} notifications`}>
```

---

### 5. Button + Loading Spinner

**Pattern**: Show async operation in progress

**✅ Recommended (composition):**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'

<Button disabled={isLoading}>
  {isLoading && <Loader2 className="mr-2 h-4 w-4 animate-spin" />}
  {isLoading ? 'Submitting...' : 'Submit'}
</Button>
```

**❌ Current implementation (variant-based):**
```tsx
// This design system has "loading" as a variant — DON'T DO THIS
<Button variant="loading">Submit</Button>
```

**Why composition wins:**
- Loading state is boolean, not a visual variant
- Text can change based on state ("Submitting..." vs. "Submit")
- Spinner can be any component (not hardcoded)
- Button is disabled during loading (correct UX)

**Accessibility**: Update `aria-busy` attribute:
```tsx
<Button aria-busy={isLoading} disabled={isLoading}>
```

---

## Button Group Compositions

### 6. ButtonGroup (Related Actions)

**Pattern**: Multiple related actions displayed together

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'

<div className="flex gap-2">
  <Button variant="outline">Bold</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Italic</Button>
  <Button variant="outline">Underline</Button>
</div>
```

**For segmented control (only one active)**:
```tsx
<div className="inline-flex rounded-md shadow-sm" role="group">
  <Button
    variant={view === 'grid' ? 'default' : 'outline'}
    className="rounded-r-none"
    onClick={() => setView('grid')}
  >
    Grid
  </Button>
  <Button
    variant={view === 'list' ? 'default' : 'outline'}
    className="rounded-none"
    onClick={() => setView('list')}
  >
    List
  </Button>
  <Button
    variant={view === 'table' ? 'default' : 'outline'}
    className="rounded-l-none"
    onClick={() => setView('table')}
  >
    Table
  </Button>
</div>
```

**Accessibility**: Use `role="group"` and `aria-label`:
```tsx
<div role="group" aria-label="View options">
```

---

## Container Compositions

### 7. Dialog + Button

**Pattern**: Buttons trigger and close dialogs

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from '@/components/ui/dialog'

<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button>Open Settings</Button>
  </DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Settings</DialogTitle>
    </DialogHeader>
    {/* Dialog content */}
    <DialogFooter>
      <Button variant="outline" onClick={handleClose}>Cancel</Button>
      <Button onClick={handleSave}>Save Changes</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- `DialogTrigger` manages dialog open/close state
- `asChild` avoids extra wrapper elements
- Footer buttons follow right-aligned pattern (Cancel left, Confirm right)
- Primary action ("Save Changes") is visually weighted (default variant)

---

### 8. Form + Button

**Pattern**: Form submission and reset actions

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'

<form onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
  {/* Form fields */}
  
  <div className="flex gap-4 justify-end mt-6">
    <Button type="button" variant="outline" onClick={handleReset}>
      Reset
    </Button>
    <Button type="submit" disabled={!isValid}>
      Submit
    </Button>
  </div>
</form>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- `type="submit"` triggers form submission (not onClick)
- `type="button"` prevents accidental form submission on reset
- `disabled={!isValid}` prevents invalid submissions
- Right-aligned buttons follow dialog pattern

**Accessibility**: Use `<form>` element (not `<div>`):
```tsx
<form onSubmit={handleSubmit} aria-labelledby="form-title">
  <h2 id="form-title">Create Account</h2>
  {/* fields */}
</form>
```

---

### 9. Card + Button

**Pattern**: Call-to-action in content cards

**✅ Recommended:**
```tsx
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { Card, CardContent, CardFooter, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card'

<Card>
  <CardHeader>
    <CardTitle>Premium Plan</CardTitle>
  </CardHeader>
  <CardContent>
    <p>Get access to all features...</p>
  </CardContent>
  <CardFooter className="flex gap-2 justify-end">
    <Button variant="outline">Learn More</Button>
    <Button>Upgrade Now</Button>
  </CardFooter>
</Card>
```

**Why this pattern works:**
- Footer separates actions from content
- Right-aligned buttons follow standard pattern
- Primary action ("Upgrade Now") is visually prominent

---

## Anti-Patterns (What NOT to Do)

### ❌ Variant Explosion

**Don't create variants for every composition:**
```tsx
// BAD — too many variants
<Button variant="with-icon" />
<Button variant="with-badge" />
<Button variant="with-dropdown" />
<Button variant="icon-left" />
<Button variant="icon-right" />
<Button variant="loading" />
```

**Do use composition instead:**
```tsx
// GOOD — compose simple components
<Button><Icon />Text</Button>
<Button><Badge />Text</Button>
<DropdownMenu><Button /></DropdownMenu>
```

---

### ❌ Deep Nesting

**Don't nest too deeply:**
```tsx
// BAD — hard to read and debug
<Dialog>
  <Form>
    <Card>
      <Tooltip>
        <Button>
          <Badge>
            <Icon />
          </Badge>
        </Button>
      </Tooltip>
    </Card>
  </Form>
</Dialog>
```

**Do flatten when possible:**
```tsx
// GOOD — extract nested components
const SubmitButton = () => (
  <Tooltip content="Submit form">
    <Button type="submit">
      <Icon />
      Submit
    </Button>
  </Tooltip>
)

<Dialog>
  <Form>
    <Card>
      <SubmitButton />
    </Card>
  </Form>
</Dialog>
```

---

### ❌ Missing Accessibility

**Don't forget `aria-*` attributes:**
```tsx
// BAD — no accessibility
<Button size="icon">
  <X />
</Button>
```

**Do add labels and roles:**
```tsx
// GOOD — accessible
<Button size="icon" aria-label="Close dialog">
  <X />
</Button>
```

---

## Composition Rules Summary

| Rule | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **Prefer composition over variants** | More flexible, fewer variants to maintain |
| **Use `asChild` prop** | Avoids extra wrapper elements, preserves accessibility |
| **Always label icon-only buttons** | `aria-label` + Tooltip for full accessibility |
| **Right-align dialog/modal actions** | Consistent with platform conventions |
| **Primary action on the right** | Cancel/secondary on left, confirm/primary on right |
| **Disable buttons during async operations** | Prevent double-submissions, indicate loading state |
| **Use `type="submit"` in forms** | Enables Enter key submission, correct semantics |
| **Extract complex compositions** | Improves readability, enables reuse |

---

## Recommendations for Full System

This pattern file was generated from a **single component validation** (Button). For a complete design system, add:

1. **All component composition patterns** — Input + Label, Select + DropdownMenu, Checkbox + Label, etc.
2. **Layout composition patterns** — Sidebar + Main, Header + Content + Footer, Grid + Card
3. **Data display compositions** — Table + Pagination, List + Filter, Chart + Legend
4. **Navigation compositions** — Navbar + Menu, Tabs + TabPanel, Breadcrumb + Link
5. **Feedback compositions** — Toast + Button, Alert + CloseButton, Banner + Action
6. **Form compositions** — FormField + Input + Error, FormGroup + Legend + Checkbox
7. **Advanced patterns** — Command Palette, Multi-Step Wizard, Data Table with Actions

---
